{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105662081?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/threalwinky", "id": 105662081, "login": "threalwinky", "node_id": "U_kgDOBkxGgQ", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/threalwinky", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-43526", "cvss": {"score": 8.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:P/VC:N/VI:N/VA:N/SC:L/SI:L/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "## Summary

QQBot reply media URL handling could trigger SSRF and re-upload fetched bytes.

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`
- Ecosystem: npm
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.12`
- Patched versions: `>= 2026.4.12`

## Impact

QQBot reply media URLs could be treated as trusted media sources, allowing SSRF fetches whose returned bytes were then re-uploaded through the channel.

## Technical Details

The fix routes QQBot remote media fetches through SSRF-guarded media fetching and explicit URL allowlist policy.

## Fix

The issue was fixed in #63495 and #65788. The first stable tag containing the fix is `v2026.4.12`, and `openclaw@2026.4.14` includes the fix.

## Fix Commit(s)

- `08ae021d1f4f02e0ca5fd8a3b9659291c1ecf95a`
- `ddb7a8dd80b8d5dd04aafa44ce7a4354b568bb2d`
- PR: #63495, #65788

## Release Process Note

Users should upgrade to `openclaw` 2026.4.12 or newer. The latest npm release, `2026.4.14`, already includes the fix.

## Credits

Thanks to @threalwinky for reporting this issue.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00251, "percentile": 0.16176}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-17T21:57:31Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-43526"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-05T12:16:18Z", "published_at": "2026-04-17T21:57:31Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63495", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/65788", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/08ae021d1f42905a85a550813c0d95169b171a6c", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ddb7a8dd80b8d5dd04aafa44ce7a4354b568bb2d", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-43526", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/08ae021d1f4f02e0ca5fd8a3b9659291c1ecf95a", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-server-side-request-forgery-via-qqbot-reply-media-url-handling", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories/GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "OpenClaw: QQBot reply media URL handling could trigger SSRF and re-upload fetched bytes", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-08T01:32:11Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2767-2q9v-9326", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.4.12", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.4.12"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OpenClaw: QQBot reply media URL handling could trigger SSRF and re-upload fetched bytes
